runs.context_blueprint: {"language": "Python", "build_system": "pip", "package_manager": "pip", "build_commands": ["pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt", "pip install --no-cache-dir -e .", "python manage.py collectstatic --noinput", "python manage.py migrate --noinput"], "install_commands": ["apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential libpq-dev libffi-dev libssl-dev ca-certificates", "rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*"], "runtime_requirements": {"language_version": "3.12", "system_packages": ["build-essential", "libpq-dev", "libffi-dev", "libssl-dev", "ca-certificates"]}, "repo_type": "web_service", "base_image": "python:3.12.13-bookworm", "base_image_rationale": "Chosen from the official images list to provide a concrete Python 3.12 environment compatible with Django/Taiga backend without relying on non-official images.", "pitfalls": ["Taiga backend relies on external services (PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ) configured via docker-compose; ensure runtime environment variables and services are available.", "No slim variant available for Python in the listed official images; the resulting image may be larger.", "Front-end and real-time components (taiga-front, taiga-events) run in separate containers; this blueprint targets the taiga-back backend container only.", "Building psycopg2 and other dependencies may require libpq-dev and compiler toolchains; ensure system packages are installed before pip install."], "notes": "This blueprint focuses on the taiga-back (Django) backend. Use the provided docker-compose files to orchestrate the full Taiga stack, including taiga-front, taiga-events, taiga-async, and RabbitMQ. Environment variables like SECRET_KEY, DB credentials, and hostnames must be supplied at runtime."}
runs.final_dockerfile:
# Taiga backend Dockerfile
# Builds the Taiga Django backend from source using Python 3.12

FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm

# Environment optimizations
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        build-essential \
        libpq-dev \
        libffi-dev \
        libssl-dev \
        ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Working directory inside the image
WORKDIR /taiga-back

# Build tooling
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -U pip setuptools wheel

# Copy the project source
COPY . .

# Install dependencies if a requirements.txt is present
RUN if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt; fi

# Install the package in editable mode if packaging is available
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -e . || true

# Install Gunicorn for production serving
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir gunicorn

# Expose the default Taiga backend port
EXPOSE 8000

# Start the Django application using Gunicorn for production-like behavior
CMD ["gunicorn", "taiga.wsgi:application", "--bind", "0.0.0.0:8000", "--workers", "3"]

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Taiga backend Dockerfile
# Builds the Taiga Django backend from source using Python 3.12

FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm

# Environment optimizations
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        build-essential \
        libpq-dev \
        libffi-dev \
        libssl-dev \
        ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Working directory inside the image
WORKDIR /taiga-back

# Copy dependency manifest and install dependencies first (better cache)
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy the full source code
COPY . .

# Install the package in editable mode (as per blueprint)
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -e .

# Build project artifacts (static files) and apply migrations
RUN python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
RUN python manage.py migrate --noinput

# Expose the default Taiga backend port
EXPOSE 8000

# Start the Django application using Gunicorn for production-like behavior
CMD ["gunicorn", "taiga.wsgi:application", "--bind", "0.0.0.0:8000", "--workers", "3"]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Taiga backend Dockerfile
# Builds the Taiga Django backend from source using Python 3.12

FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm

# Environment optimizations
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        build-essential \
        libpq-dev \
        libffi-dev \
        libssl-dev \
        ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Working directory inside the image
WORKDIR /taiga-back

# Build tooling
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -U pip setuptools wheel

# Copy the project source
COPY . .

# Install the project in editable mode if packaging is available
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -e . || true

# Install Gunicorn for production serving
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir gunicorn

# Prepare entrypoint script (handle migrations and static files at startup)
COPY docker-entrypoint.sh /docker-entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /docker-entrypoint.sh

# Expose the default Taiga backend port
EXPOSE 8000

# Start the container with the entrypoint
ENTRYPOINT ["/docker-entrypoint.sh"]
"}

    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Working directory inside the image

# Build tooling
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -U pip setuptools wheel

# Copy the project source
COPY . .

# Install dependencies if a requirements.txt is present
RUN if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt; fi

# Install the package in editable mode if packaging is available
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -e . || true

# Install Gunicorn for production serving
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir gunicorn

# Expose the default Taiga backend port
EXPOSE 8000

# Start the Django application using Gunicorn for production-like behavior
CMD ["gunicorn", "taiga.wsgi:application", "--bind", "0.0.0.0:8000", "--workers", "3"]
"}

artifacts.content:
# Taiga backend Dockerfile
# Builds the Taiga Django backend from source using Python 3.12

FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm

# Environment optimizations
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        build-essential \
        libpq-dev \
        libffi-dev \
        libssl-dev \
        ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Working directory inside the image
WORKDIR /taiga-back

# Build tooling
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -U pip setuptools wheel

# Copy the project source
COPY . .

# Install dependencies if a requirements.txt is present
RUN if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt; fi

# Install the package in editable mode if packaging is available
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -e . || true

# Install Gunicorn for production serving
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir gunicorn

# Expose the default Taiga backend port
EXPOSE 8000

# Start the Django application using Gunicorn for production-like behavior
CMD ["gunicorn", "taiga.wsgi:application", "--bind", "0.0.0.0:8000", "--workers", "3"]
